disclaimer: Educational fitness content, not medical advice. Stop any movement that causes sharp pain, chest pain, dizziness, or shortness of breath, and clear new exercise with your doctor first — especially with heart, joint, blood-pressure, or pregnancy concerns.
clearance: Let's start this the safe way. Before your first workout, it's worth a quick word with your doctor — especially if you have a heart condition, diabetes, high blood pressure, joint pain, or you get chest pain, breathlessness, or dizziness when you're active. Most people get the green light to start with gentle walking. This takes 2 minutes and means you can move with total confidence. 💚
stop_signs: Breathlessness that feels severe or scary (not just “puffed”)
stop_note: If any of these don't settle quickly with rest, call your local emergency number. These are rare — knowing them just means you can move with confidence.
expectations: Here's the honest deal: what you eat drives the number on the scale — that's why SnapCal tracks it. Movement is what makes that weight loss healthy: it protects your muscle so you lose fat (not strength), guards your joints and heart, and lifts your mood and energy this week. Don't chase calories burned — chase how good you feel. 💪
global_rules: Warm up 3-5 min before and cool down/stretch 3-5 min after every session.
Breathe out on the effort, never hold your breath.
Move through a pain-free range only; 'challenging but doable' is the target, not 'maximum'.
Progress by adding reps first, then sets, then resistance — never all at once.
Use the Talk Test: if you can talk but not sing, you've nailed the right effort.

walking.title: Walking is your #1 move
walking.lead: At a higher weight every step burns close to double — so about 7,000 steps a day works out to roughly 500-600 calories. It's your biggest, most joint-friendly lever, and it costs nothing: no gym, no impact, just steps. Park farther out, take the long way, walk while you're on the phone.
walking.neat: Sitting for hours quietly switches off fat-burning in your legs — breaking it up beats any single workout. Aim for a 2-3 minute Desk-Break every 30-60 minutes.

tiers.name: Start Here
tiers.tagline: Seated & standing-supported. Joint-friendly. No floor work.
tiers.audience: Heavy or very deconditioned beginners. Everything is done seated or standing while holding a sturdy chair, counter, or wall — nothing requires getting down to or up from the floor.
equipment: Counter or chair back for support
tiers.weekly: 3-5 days/week (start at 3). Strength days (A & C) + a mobility day (B), with short easy walks + desk-break snacks on the in-between days. Rest or an easy walk on weekends.

sessions.name: Session A — Full-Body Seated + Standing Strength

exercises.name: Seated Marching
exercises.cue: Sit tall, lift one knee then the other like a slow march, swinging arms gently.
exercises.easier: Lift each foot just an inch off the floor, or alternate small heel raises.

exercises.name: Seated Shoulder & Arm Circles
exercises.cue: Roll shoulders in slow, smooth circles; keep them relaxed, not shrugged.
exercises.easier: Tiny circles, or one shoulder at a time.

exercises.name: Sit-to-Stand (Chair Squat)
exercises.cue: Scoot forward, press through your heels, push hands on thighs if needed, stand fully, then sit slow.
exercises.easier: Push up using the armrests, or just lift your hips an inch before sitting back.

exercises.name: Wall Push-Ups
exercises.cue: Hands on wall at shoulder height, body straight, bend elbows to bring chest toward wall, press back.
exercises.easier: Stand closer to the wall (more upright = easier); fewer reps.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Extensions
exercises.cue: Sit tall, straighten one knee, squeeze the thigh, lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Extend only halfway, or alternate legs.

exercises.name: Seated Toe & Heel Raises
exercises.cue: Lift toes up, then heels up, rolling through the foot — keeps ankles and circulation working.
exercises.easier: Toes-only or heels-only, fewer reps.

sessions.cooldown: Seated neck side-bends, overhead reach, slow seated forward fold — hold each 15-20 sec.

sessions.name: Session B — Mobility & Gentle Range of Motion

exercises.name: Seated Neck Mobility
exercises.cue: Slow and small — ear toward shoulder, then look over each shoulder; never force or roll back.
exercises.easier: Reduce range to whatever is fully comfortable.

exercises.name: Seated Cat-Cow
exercises.cue: Hands on thighs; round the back and tuck chin, then arch and lift the chest — breathe with it.
exercises.easier: Smaller spinal movement.

exercises.name: Seated Torso Rotation
exercises.cue: Cross arms on chest, rotate gently to look behind you, return to center, other side.
exercises.easier: Smaller turn.

exercises.name: Seated Hamstring Stretch
exercises.cue: Extend one leg, heel on floor, sit tall and hinge slightly forward for a gentle stretch.
exercises.easier: Soft bend in the knee; lean less.

exercises.name: Seated Ankle Circles
exercises.cue: Lift one foot slightly and draw slow circles with the toes.
exercises.easier: Heel on the floor, circle just the front of the foot.

exercises.name: Standing Supported Calf Stretch
exercises.cue: Hold the counter, step one foot back with heel down, lean gently to stretch the calf.
exercises.easier: Smaller step back, or a seated calf press.

sessions.cooldown: Seated slow deep breathing × 5, shoulders dropping on each exhale.

sessions.name: Session C — Standing Strength & Balance (supported)

exercises.name: March in Place (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold the counter, march lifting knees to a comfortable height, tall posture.
exercises.easier: Smaller steps, or lift heels only; or seated marching.

exercises.name: Counter-Supported Mini Squats
exercises.cue: Hold the counter, feet hip-width, bend knees a few inches like sitting back, then stand.
exercises.easier: Bend only an inch or two; or sit-to-stands from a chair.

exercises.name: Standing Heel Raises (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, rise onto the balls of your feet, pause, lower slowly.
exercises.easier: Smaller lift; or seated with hands pressing on thighs.

exercises.name: Standing Side Leg Lift (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, body tall, lift one leg out to the side a few inches, lower with control.
exercises.easier: Lift only slightly; or seated knee-out openings.

exercises.name: Standing Leg-Back (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, squeeze the glute and press one leg straight back a few inches, don't arch.
exercises.easier: Small range; toes lightly brushing the floor.

exercises.name: Standing Weight Shifts (balance)
exercises.cue: Hold support, slowly shift weight side to side onto each foot.
exercises.easier: Smaller shifts, two hands on support.

sessions.cooldown: Standing supported calf stretch + seated hamstring stretch, 20 sec each.

tiers.name: Gentle — Senior-Friendly
tiers.tagline: Balance, mobility & light strength. Standing-supported & seated. Fall-proof your body.
tiers.audience: Built for older adults and anyone who wants a kind, steady pace. The goals here are balance and fall-prevention, staying mobile, and keeping muscle and bone strong — with a sturdy chair or counter always within reach. Nothing requires getting onto the floor.
equipment: Optional: light 1-3 lb dumbbells or filled water bottles
tiers.weekly: 3-4 days/week. Two strength-and-balance days (A & C) and one mobility day (B), with easy daily walks on the in-between days. Never rush — slow and steady builds the most.
notes: Balance is a skill you can rebuild at any age — a few minutes of practice each session meaningfully lowers fall risk and keeps you independent.
Always keep one hand near support until a balance move feels easy, then try a fingertip touch. Safety first, always.
Light resistance + daily protein protects muscle and bone as the years go on. Gentle and consistent beats hard and occasional.

sessions.name: Session A — Strength & Balance (supported)

exercises.name: Sit-to-Stand
exercises.cue: From a sturdy chair, scoot forward, press through your heels and stand tall, then lower slowly with control. This is the #1 move for staying independent.
exercises.easier: Push off the armrests, or stand to just before fully up and sit back.

exercises.name: Counter-Supported Mini Squats
exercises.cue: Hold the counter, feet hip-width, bend the knees a few inches like sitting back, then stand and squeeze.
exercises.easier: Bend only an inch or two.

exercises.name: Standing Heel-to-Toe Raises (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, rise onto the balls of your feet, lower, then rock back onto your heels lifting the toes.
exercises.easier: Heels-only or toes-only, fewer reps.

exercises.name: Wall Push-Ups
exercises.cue: Hands on the wall at shoulder height, body straight, bend the elbows to bring your chest toward the wall, press back.
exercises.easier: Stand closer to the wall (more upright = easier).

exercises.name: Standing Side Leg Lift (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, stand tall, lift one leg out to the side a few inches and lower with control — builds hip strength for steady walking.
exercises.easier: Smaller lift.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Balance (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold the counter and lift one foot just off the floor; stand tall and breathe. Progress to a fingertip touch over time.
exercises.easier: Both hands on support; lift the foot only slightly.

sessions.cooldown: Seated hamstring stretch + standing supported calf stretch, 20 sec each; slow shoulder rolls.

sessions.name: Session B — Mobility & Flexibility

exercises.name: Seated Cat-Cow
exercises.cue: Hands on thighs; round the back and tuck the chin, then arch and lift the chest. Breathe with the movement.
exercises.easier: Smaller range.

exercises.name: Seated Torso Rotation
exercises.cue: Cross arms on the chest, turn gently to look behind you, return to center, then the other side. Keeps the spine supple.
exercises.easier: Smaller turn.

exercises.name: Seated Neck Mobility
exercises.cue: Slow and small — ear toward the shoulder, then look over each shoulder. Never force or roll the head back.
exercises.easier: Reduce range to fully comfortable.

exercises.name: Standing Supported Hip Circles
exercises.cue: Hold support, hands on hips, draw slow gentle circles with the hips to loosen the lower back.
exercises.easier: Tiny circles.

exercises.name: Seated Ankle Circles & Foot Rolls
exercises.cue: Lift one foot slightly and draw slow circles, then roll through toe-to-heel. Keeps ankles mobile for balance.
exercises.easier: Heel on the floor, circle just the front of the foot.

exercises.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
exercises.cue: Forearm on the door frame, gently step through to open the chest and shoulders — undoes years of forward posture.
exercises.easier: Lower the arm; smaller step.

sessions.cooldown: Slow deep breathing × 5, shoulders dropping on each exhale.

sessions.name: Session C — Standing Strength & Posture (supported)

exercises.name: March in Place (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, march lifting the knees to a comfortable height, tall posture, gentle arm swing.
exercises.easier: Smaller steps or lift heels only.

exercises.name: Standing Leg-Back (supported)
exercises.cue: Hold support, squeeze the glute and press one leg straight back a few inches — don't arch the back.
exercises.easier: Small range, toes brushing the floor.

exercises.name: Light Dumbbell or Bottle Shoulder Press
exercises.cue: Seated or standing tall, press light weights from the shoulders to overhead and lower slow. Brace the core, don't arch.
exercises.easier: One arm at a time, or no weight.

exercises.name: Standing Row with Band or Bottles
exercises.cue: Hinge slightly, pull the elbows back and squeeze the shoulder blades. Best posture move there is.
exercises.easier: Lighter resistance, seated.

exercises.name: Standing Weight Shifts (balance)
exercises.cue: Hold support, slowly shift your weight fully onto each foot, pausing to feel steady.
exercises.easier: Smaller shifts, two hands on support.

exercises.name: Chin Tucks (posture)
exercises.cue: Gently draw the chin straight back to make a 'double chin', hold a second, release. Counters tech-neck and keeps you tall.
exercises.easier: Smaller, gentler movement.

sessions.cooldown: Standing supported calf stretch + cross-body shoulder stretch, 20 sec each.

tiers.name: Intermediate — Build & Burn
tiers.tagline: Standing full-body strength + low-impact cardio. Light dumbbells. More challenge, knees protected.
tiers.audience: For when Start Here feels easy and you can move comfortably standing. This steps up to standing full-body strength with light-to-moderate dumbbells and low-impact cardio intervals. Still zero jumping or running — all cardio stays joint-friendly to keep your knees happy.
tiers.weekly: 3-4 days/week: two strength days (A & C) and one cardio day (B), keeping your daily walks and desk-breaks going on top. Leave a day between the two strength days.
notes: Pair this with high protein and you're recomposing — losing fat while building muscle so you finish solid, not skinny.
Progress the smart way: add reps first, then a set, then a little weight — never all three at once. Leave 1-2 reps in the tank.
Low-impact still gets your heart up: intervals of brisk marching, step-touch, and incline walking spike the burn without pounding your joints.

sessions.name: Session A — Lower Body & Core (low impact)

exercises.name: Goblet Squat to Chair
exercises.cue: Hold a dumbbell at your chest, sit back to lightly tap a chair, drive through the heels to stand and squeeze. Knees track over the toes.
exercises.easier: No weight, or higher chair / smaller depth.

exercises.name: Standing Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.cue: Soft knees, push the hips straight back lowering the weights down your thighs, feel the hamstrings, then squeeze the glutes to stand. Flat back.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, smaller range.

exercises.name: Supported Reverse Lunge (low impact)
exercises.cue: Hold support, step one foot back and lower gently, press through the front heel to return. Step back, never jump.
exercises.easier: Smaller step and shallow bend; or a standing split-stance squat.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raises
exercises.cue: Rise onto the balls of the feet, pause at the top, lower slow. Add a dumbbell when easy.
exercises.easier: Bodyweight, hold support.

exercises.name: Standing Pallof Press (band, anti-rotation)
exercises.cue: Band from the side at chest height, press straight out and resist the twist. Brace the abs hard — a strong, protective core.
exercises.easier: Stand closer to the anchor; fewer reps.

exercises.name: Dead Bug (on bench or standing march)
exercises.cue: If comfortable on a bench: extend opposite arm and leg slowly, keeping the low back pressed down. Prefer standing? Do slow high-knee marches with a tight core.
exercises.easier: Move just the legs, or do standing marches.

sessions.cooldown: Standing quad stretch (hold support) 20 sec/leg, hamstring stretch 20 sec/leg, hip-flexor lunge stretch 20 sec/side.

sessions.name: Session B — Low-Impact Cardio Intervals

exercises.name: Warm-Up March
exercises.cue: March in place or walk, gradually picking up the pace, arms swinging, until you feel warm and loose.
exercises.easier: Slower, seated marching.

exercises.name: Brisk March / Step-Touch Intervals
exercises.cue: Go brisk — high marching or side step-touches with arm pumps — then ease off. Talk test: brisk = can talk but not sing.
exercises.easier: Lower the knees / smaller steps; extend the easy portion.

exercises.name: Standing Punch-Outs
exercises.cue: Light, controlled punches forward and across, staying light on the feet with a braced core. Gets the heart up, spares the knees.
exercises.easier: Slower pace, no reaching.

exercises.name: Incline Walk or Marching Finisher
exercises.cue: If you have a treadmill incline or a hill, walk it steady; otherwise a steady purposeful march. Heart rate up, joints calm.
exercises.easier: Flat, shorter, slower.

sessions.cooldown: Slow walking 2-3 min to bring the heart rate down, then calf + hamstring + chest stretches 20-30 sec each.

sessions.name: Session C — Upper Body & Core

exercises.name: Dumbbell Floor or Bench Press
exercises.cue: Press the dumbbells up over the chest, lower under control until the elbows reach the floor/bench. Shoulders down.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, or incline push-ups against a counter.

exercises.name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.cue: Brace a hand and knee on a bench (or hand on your thigh), pull the dumbbell to the hip, squeeze the back, lower slow.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight; support the chest more.

exercises.name: Seated or Standing Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.cue: Dumbbells at ear height, press up and slightly together, lower to the ears. Brace the core, don't arch.
exercises.easier: One arm at a time, lighter weight.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart or Reverse Fly
exercises.cue: Arms out to the sides, squeeze the shoulder blades, lead with the pinkies. Posture gold after a desk day.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, smaller range.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl + Overhead Triceps
exercises.cue: Curl without swinging, then one dumbbell overhead and lower behind the head for triceps. Control both directions.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, alternate arms.

exercises.name: Counter Plank
exercises.cue: Hands on a sturdy counter or bench, body one straight line, squeeze glutes and abs, breathe. Higher hands = easier.
exercises.easier: Hands on a higher surface; shorter holds.

sessions.cooldown: Doorway chest stretch 20-30 sec, cross-body + overhead triceps stretch 20 sec/arm, gentle cat-cow ×5.

tiers.name: Advanced — Strong & Lean
tiers.tagline: Higher-volume strength supersets + hard low-impact conditioning. For seasoned movers, joints respected.
tiers.audience: For experienced, well-conditioned movers who want a real challenge. Bigger lifts, supersets, and demanding low-impact conditioning — still no jumping or running, because smart training protects the knees for the long game. If a move ever causes joint pain, scale back to the Intermediate version.
equipment: A full set of dumbbells (or access to machines/cables)
Optional: bike, rower, or treadmill with incline for conditioning
tiers.weekly: 4 days/week: Lower (A), Upper (C), a conditioning day (B), and a repeat of your weaker of A/C — or run A/B/C/B. Keep a hard day away from another hard day for the same muscles. Daily steps stay non-negotiable.
notes: Train hard, recover harder: progressive overload only works if you sleep, hit your protein, and don't redline every set. Leave ~1 rep in the tank on most sets, push the last set.
Supersets (two moves back-to-back) keep the heart rate up and pack more work into less time — great for fat loss while you build.
Knee-safe by design: load the hips with hinges, split-stance and supported work; get your intensity from bike/row/incline, never from impact.

sessions.name: Session A — Lower Body Power (low impact)

exercises.name: Goblet or Dumbbell Squat
exercises.cue: Hold weight at the chest (or dumbbells at the sides), sit back and down to a comfortable depth, drive up through the heels. Controlled, deep as your knees allow pain-free.
exercises.easier: Squat to a higher box, lighter load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.cue: Soft knees, hips back, lower the weights down the legs with a flat back until you feel the hamstrings, squeeze the glutes to stand. This is your big knee-friendly strength builder.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, smaller range.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat (rear foot on bench)
exercises.cue: Rear foot up on a bench, drop straight down on the front leg, drive through the front heel. Hold support if needed — controlled, never bouncing.
exercises.easier: Rear foot on the floor (split stance), shallower, hold support.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust or Glute Bridge (weighted)
exercises.cue: Shoulders on a bench (or back on the floor), weight across the hips, drive the hips up and squeeze the glutes hard at the top, lower slow.
exercises.easier: Bodyweight, floor bridge.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise (weighted)
exercises.cue: Hold a dumbbell, rise high onto the balls of the feet, pause, lower slow for a stretch.
exercises.easier: Bodyweight, hold support.

exercises.name: Hanging or Captain's-Chair Knee Raise
exercises.cue: Lift the knees toward the chest with control, no swinging — lower abs drive it. Use a captain's chair to spare the grip and back.
exercises.easier: Seated knee raises on a bench.

sessions.cooldown: Couch/hip-flexor stretch 30 sec/side, hamstring + quad stretch 30 sec/leg, deep breathing ×5.

sessions.name: Session B — Hard Low-Impact Conditioning

exercises.name: Warm-Up Build
exercises.cue: Bike, row, or brisk incline walk, building from easy to moderate so you're fully warm before the hard work.
exercises.easier: Shorter, easier.

exercises.name: Intervals: Bike / Row / Incline
exercises.cue: Hard = breathing heavy, can only get a word out; easy = recover. All seated or walking — max heart rate, zero pounding.
exercises.easier: 6 rounds, 20 sec hard / 90 sec easy.

exercises.name: Loaded Carry (farmer's walk)
exercises.cue: Heavy dumbbell in each hand, stand tall, brace the core, walk smooth and controlled. Total-body strength and conditioning in one.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, shorter distance.

exercises.name: Steady Finisher
exercises.cue: Bike, row, or incline walk at a strong but sustainable pace to finish the tank off.
exercises.easier: Shorter, lower intensity.

sessions.cooldown: Easy 3-5 min cool-down on the bike/walk, then full lower-body stretch 30 sec each.

sessions.name: Session C — Upper Body Strength (supersets)

exercises.name: Superset 1a: Dumbbell Bench/Floor Press
exercises.cue: Press strong over the chest, lower under control. Pair back-to-back with 1b for density.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, incline push-ups.

exercises.name: Superset 1b: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.cue: Pull the dumbbell to the hip, squeeze the back hard, lower slow. Then rest and repeat the pair.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, more chest support.

exercises.name: Superset 2a: Standing Overhead Press
exercises.cue: Brace the core and glutes, press the dumbbells overhead without arching, lower to the ears.
exercises.easier: Seated, lighter, one arm at a time.

exercises.name: Superset 2b: Lat Pulldown or Band Pulldown
exercises.cue: Drive the elbows down and back to bring the bar to the upper chest, squeeze the lats, control the return.
exercises.easier: Lighter, supported single-arm pulldown.

exercises.name: Superset 3a: Incline Curl
exercises.cue: Arms hanging back on an incline bench, curl with control, no swing, slow negative.
exercises.easier: Standing curls, lighter.

exercises.name: Superset 3b: Overhead or Cable Triceps Extension
exercises.cue: Elbows steady, extend to straight, squeeze the triceps, control back. Only the forearms move.
exercises.easier: Lighter band or weight.

exercises.name: Face Pull (posture finisher)
exercises.cue: Pull toward the forehead with elbows high, squeeze the rear shoulders. Bulletproofs the shoulders against all that pressing.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, seated.

sessions.cooldown: Doorway chest stretch 30 sec, lat stretch 20 sec/side, cross-body + overhead triceps stretch 20 sec/arm.

tiers.name: Iron — Upper Body & Core
tiers.tagline: Gym strength to build muscle while you lose fat. Upper body + core, knee-safe.
tiers.audience: For when you're ready to lift. This builds and protects muscle so you lose FAT and stay solid — not skinny. Every move is upper-body or core, done seated, machine-based, or standing-supported to keep your knees safe: no squats, lunges, running, or jumping. Start lighter than you think and own the form first.
equipment: Machines if you have them (chest press, lat pulldown, seated row, cable)
A resistance band subs for any cable move
tiers.weekly: 2-3 non-consecutive days/week (e.g. Mon/Thu, or Mon/Wed/Fri). Leave at least a day between sessions that hit the same muscles. Keep your daily walks + desk-breaks going on top.
notes: Why this matters: lifting + high protein is your recomposition engine — you lose FAT while keeping and building muscle, so you finish solid, not saggy. This is your best defense against loose skin.
Form before weight, always. Start light, own the movement, and leave 1-2 reps 'in the tank' each set. Add a little weight only once every set is clean at the top of the rep range.
Knee-safe by design: upper-body and core only, seated/machine/supported. Breathe OUT on the effort — never hold your breath.

sessions.name: Push — Chest, Shoulders & Triceps

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press (or Dumbbell Bench Press)
exercises.cue: Seated, handles at chest height. Press smoothly to nearly-straight arms (don't lock hard), squeeze the chest, lower under control for 2-3 sec. Shoulders down and back.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, or wall/incline push-ups for 2 sets of as many as stay clean.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.cue: Back supported, dumbbells at ear height, press up and slightly together, lower to the ears. Brace your core, don't arch the low back.
exercises.easier: Lighter dumbbells, one arm at a time, or a seated band overhead press.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press or Machine Chest Fly
exercises.cue: Slight incline to hit the upper chest. Press/squeeze with control — feel the chest work, not the shoulders.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, fewer sets, or a band chest squeeze.

exercises.name: Cable or Band Triceps Pushdown
exercises.cue: Elbows pinned to your sides, push down to straight arms, squeeze the triceps, return slow. Only the forearms move.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, or seated overhead dumbbell extension.

exercises.name: Seated Overhead Dumbbell Triceps Extension
exercises.cue: One dumbbell in both hands overhead, lower behind your head with elbows pointing up, press back up. Slow and controlled.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, or a band triceps press.

sessions.cooldown: Doorway chest stretch 20-30 sec, overhead triceps stretch 20 sec/arm, slow shoulder rolls.

sessions.name: Pull — Back & Biceps

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (or Band Pulldown)
exercises.cue: Pull the bar to your upper chest by driving the elbows down and back, squeeze the shoulder blades, return slow. Lead with the back, not the arms.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, or a seated band pulldown / supported single-arm row.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (or Dumbbell Bench Row)
exercises.cue: Tall chest, pull the handle to your belly, squeeze the shoulder blades together, control it back out. Don't rock with the low back.
exercises.easier: Lighter weight, one arm at a time braced on a bench or your thigh.

exercises.name: Machine Reverse Fly or Band Pull-Apart
exercises.cue: Arms out to the sides, squeeze the shoulder blades, lead with the pinkies. Great for posture after a desk day.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, smaller range.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Biceps Curl
exercises.cue: Elbows at your sides, curl without swinging, squeeze at the top, lower for a slow count of three.
exercises.easier: Lighter dumbbells, alternate arms, or band curls.

exercises.name: Cable or Band Face Pull
exercises.cue: Pull toward your forehead with elbows high, squeeze the rear shoulders. This is your posture move — undoes desk slump.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, seated.

sessions.cooldown: Seated lat stretch (reach overhead and lean) 20 sec/side, cross-body shoulder stretch 20 sec/arm, biceps wall stretch 20 sec/arm.

sessions.name: Core & Abs — Strong Middle

exercises.name: Seated Cable Crunch or Machine Crunch
exercises.cue: Curl your ribs toward your hips — round the spine, don't just bend at the hips. Exhale hard at the bottom and feel the abs squeeze.
exercises.easier: Band crunch anchored above you, or a seated lean-back hold.

exercises.name: Seated Knee Raises (chair or captain's chair)
exercises.cue: Sit tall, grip the seat, lift both knees toward your chest with control, lower slow without flopping. Lower abs do the work.
exercises.easier: One knee at a time, smaller lift, or braced seated marches.

exercises.name: Standing Cable/Band Pallof Press (anti-rotation)
exercises.cue: Band at chest height from the side, press straight out and resist the pull that wants to twist you. Brace the abs hard — builds a stable, protective core.
exercises.easier: Stand closer to the anchor (less resistance), fewer reps.

exercises.name: Incline or Counter Plank
exercises.cue: Hands on a sturdy counter or bench, body in one straight line, squeeze glutes and abs, breathe. No sagging hips — the higher your hands, the easier. No floor needed.
exercises.easier: Hands on a higher surface or the wall, shorter holds.

exercises.name: Standing Band Wood-Chop (gentle)
exercises.cue: Band low to one side, pull diagonally up and across with braced abs, control it back. Smooth, no jerking — obliques and rotation strength.
exercises.easier: Lighter band, smaller range, or do the Pallof press instead.

sessions.cooldown: Standing side-bend stretch 20 sec/side, gentle seated cat-cow ×5, deep breathing ×5.

desk_breaks.name: Desk-Break Snacks
desk_breaks.tagline: 2-5 minute movement breaks to interrupt long sitting.
desk_breaks.why: Breaking up 12-14 hours of sitting improves circulation, blood sugar, energy, posture, and hip/back comfort — more than one workout can offset alone.
desk_breaks.frequency: Take one every 30-60 minutes of sitting. Make the walk-around your longest, every 1-2 hours. Set a timer.

snacks.name: Stand & Reset
snacks.cue: Stand, roll shoulders back 5×, reach overhead, 3 slow breaths. Tall spine, shoulders down.

snacks.name: Calf Raises
snacks.cue: 20-30 standing calf raises by your desk — rise, squeeze, lower slow. Great for circulation.

snacks.name: Sit-to-Stands
snacks.cue: 10 stand-ups from your chair without using your hands. Press through the heels.

snacks.name: Hip-Flexor Stretch
snacks.cue: Standing lunge stretch, 30 sec per side. Tuck the tailbone, press the hip forward — undoes the 'seated hip'.

snacks.name: Posture Reset (Chin Tuck)
snacks.cue: Gently draw the chin straight back (double-chin) 5×; squeeze shoulder blades 5×. Counters 'tech neck'.

snacks.name: Neck & Shoulder Rolls
snacks.cue: 5 slow shoulder rolls back, 3 gentle neck side-bends each way. Release shoulders from the ears.

snacks.name: Walk-Around
snacks.cue: Walk to refill water, take a lap, or step outside. Bonus if you add stairs.

snacks.name: Marching in Place
snacks.cue: March in place 60-90 sec, pumping the arms — a light cardio snack to beat the afternoon dip.
